title: Bromus interruptus
taxonomy: Bromus interruptus is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This species is further classified under the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Bromeae. It belongs to the genus Bromus, which comprises over 160 species of grasses. The classification of Bromus interruptus is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Clade: Commelinids, Order: Poales, Family: Poaceae, Subfamily: Pooideae, Tribe: Bromeae, Genus: Bromus, Species: B. interruptus. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.

intro: Bromus interruptus, also known as Smooth Brome, is a perennial grass native to the grasslands and meadows of North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, providing relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Bromus interruptus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Bromus interruptus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
